gems.name: Sørvágsvatn
gems.blurb: A lake that optically appears to hover hundreds of metres above the crashing ocean.
gems.tip: Walk the Trælanípan cliff trail late afternoon for the floating-lake illusion.

gems.name: Socotra
gems.blurb: An island of alien Dragon's Blood trees found nowhere else on Earth.
gems.tip: Camp on the Diksam Plateau; bring everything — infrastructure is near zero.

gems.name: Hang Sơn Đoòng
gems.blurb: The largest cave passage on the planet — big enough to hold a city block.
gems.tip: Only one operator is permitted; book the multi-day expedition a year out.

gems.name: Chefchaouen
gems.blurb: A mountain town painted entirely in dreamy shades of blue.
gems.tip: Hike up to the Spanish Mosque for sunset over the blue maze.

gems.name: Salar de Uyuni
gems.blurb: The world's largest salt flat becomes a perfect mirror after rain.
gems.tip: Visit in the wet season for the mirror effect; sunrise reflections are unreal.

gems.name: Plitvice Lakes
gems.blurb: Sixteen terraced turquoise lakes linked by tumbling waterfalls.
gems.tip: Enter at the upper lakes early to beat the tour buses.

gems.name: Bagan
gems.blurb: A plain of over 2,000 ancient temples best seen from a balloon at dawn.
gems.tip: Find a quieter pagoda away from the main cluster for sunrise.

gems.name: Cappadocia
gems.blurb: Surreal fairy-chimney valleys under a sky full of balloons.
gems.tip: Stay in a cave hotel in Göreme; book the balloon for your first morning in case of delays.

gems.name: Jiufen
gems.blurb: A lantern-lit mountain tea town of narrow lanes and steaming street food.
gems.tip: Go on a weekday evening; try the taro balls and a-gei.

gems.name: Lofoten Islands
gems.blurb: Jagged arctic peaks dropping straight into white-sand beaches.
gems.tip: Chase the northern lights in winter or the midnight sun in summer.

gems.name: Wadi Rum
gems.blurb: Mars-red desert of towering sandstone where Bedouin still guide travellers.
gems.tip: Sleep in a bubble camp for stargazing; pair it with Petra two hours north.

gems.name: Raja Ampat
gems.blurb: The most biodiverse reefs on Earth scattered across emerald karst islands.
gems.tip: Liveaboard dive boats reach the best sites; the Wayag viewpoint hike is worth it.

gems.name: Torres del Paine
gems.blurb: Granite spires, glaciers and guanacos on Patagonia's iconic W-trek.
gems.tip: Book refugios months ahead; pack for four seasons in one day.

gems.name: Zhangjiajie
gems.blurb: Thousands of sandstone pillars that inspired Avatar's floating mountains.
gems.tip: Time the glass elevator and skywalk for early-morning mist.

gems.name: Meteora
gems.blurb: Centuries-old monasteries perched impossibly atop sheer rock towers.
gems.tip: Hike between monasteries rather than driving; sunset from the Observation Deck.

gems.name: Bay of Kotor
gems.blurb: A fjord-like bay of medieval towns ringed by dramatic mountains.
gems.tip: Climb the old fortress walls above Kotor before the cruise crowds.

gems.name: Antelope Canyon
gems.blurb: Glowing wave-like slot canyon carved by flash floods.
gems.tip: Upper canyon for light beams at midday; a guide is mandatory.

gems.name: Matera
gems.blurb: An ancient city of cave dwellings carved into a dramatic ravine.
gems.tip: Sleep in a restored cave hotel; the night-lit panorama is the highlight.

gems.name: Lake Bled
gems.blurb: A storybook island church on an emerald lake beneath a clifftop castle.
gems.tip: Row a traditional pletna boat out and ring the wishing bell at dawn.

gems.name: Pamukkale
gems.blurb: Cascading white travertine terraces filled with warm turquoise pools.
gems.tip: Combine with the Roman ruins of Hierapolis; shoes aren't allowed on the terraces.

gems.name: Huacachina
gems.blurb: A tiny palm-fringed desert oasis surrounded by towering dunes.
gems.tip: Sunset dune-buggy and sandboarding, then watch the stars from a crest.

gems.name: Lençóis Maranhenses
gems.blurb: Endless white dunes cradling thousands of rain-fed turquoise lagoons.
gems.tip: The lagoons only fill mid-year; base yourself in Barreirinhas.

gems.name: Tsingy de Bemaraha
gems.blurb: A razor-sharp limestone stone forest crossed on via-ferrata bridges.
gems.tip: Rough roads — go with a 4x4 tour in the dry season only.

gems.name: Ksamil
gems.blurb: Caribbean-clear water and tiny islands on the still-quiet Albanian Riviera.
gems.tip: Swim out to the small islets; visit nearby Butrint ruins.

gems.name: Hallstatt
gems.blurb: A pastel lakeside village wedged between mountains and a mirror-still lake.
gems.tip: Stay overnight — the village empties of day-trippers after 4pm.

gems.name: Svalbard
gems.blurb: Far-north arctic wilderness of glaciers, polar bears and endless light or dark.
gems.tip: Take a guided snowmobile safari; a rifle-carrying guide is required outside town.

gems.name: Machu Picchu
gems.blurb: The mist-wreathed Inca citadel perched on a knife-edge Andean ridge.
gems.tip: Hike the last stretch from Sun Gate at dawn; tickets sell out weeks ahead.

gems.name: Rainbow Mountain
gems.blurb: A mineral-striped ridge of impossible reds, golds and turquoise at 5,000m.
gems.tip: Acclimatise in Cusco first; start early to beat clouds and crowds.

gems.name: Iguazú Falls
gems.blurb: 275 thundering cascades straddling the jungle border of Argentina and Brazil.
gems.tip: Do the Argentine side for the Devil's Throat walkway, Brazilian for the panorama.

gems.name: Perito Moreno Glacier
gems.blurb: A vast advancing glacier that calves house-sized ice into a milky lake.
gems.tip: Take the boardwalks at midday when ice is most likely to thunder down.

gems.name: Atacama Desert
gems.blurb: The driest desert on Earth — geysers, salt lagoons and the clearest night skies.
gems.tip: Base in San Pedro; the high-altitude stargazing tours are unmatched.

gems.name: Marble Caves
gems.blurb: Swirled blue marble caverns glowing above a glacial Patagonian lake.
gems.tip: Go by kayak on a calm morning when the water is glassiest.

gems.name: Mount Roraima
gems.blurb: A flat-topped tepui rising from the clouds like a lost world.
gems.tip: A 6-day guided trek from Paraitepui; expect rain and surreal rock gardens.

gems.name: Galápagos Islands
gems.blurb: Fearless wildlife — tortoises, iguanas and boobies — on volcanic isles.
gems.tip: A small liveaboard reaches the wilder outer islands; respect the 2m rule.

gems.name: Quilotoa
gems.blurb: A vivid green crater lake ringed by a rim trail high in the Andes.
gems.tip: Walk the rim loop or hike the Quilotoa Loop villages over a few days.

gems.name: Caño Cristales
gems.blurb: The liquid rainbow — a river that blooms red, yellow and green underwater.
gems.tip: Only accessible by tour from La Macarena during the bloom season.

gems.name: Valle de Cocora
gems.blurb: Mist-draped valley spiked with the world's tallest wax palms.
gems.tip: Hike the loop from Salento early; combine with a coffee-farm tour.

gems.name: Cartagena
gems.blurb: A walled Caribbean old town of bougainvillea balconies and salsa nights.
gems.tip: Walk the ramparts at golden hour; day-trip to the Rosario Islands.

gems.name: Rio de Janeiro
gems.blurb: Mountains, beaches and Christ the Redeemer in one electric city.
gems.tip: Sunset from Sugarloaf, sunrise hike up Pedra Bonita over the favelas.

gems.name: Chapada Diamantina
gems.blurb: Table mountains, glowing caves and waterfalls deep in the Bahian interior.
gems.tip: Base in Lençóis; the Poço Azul cave glows blue at midday.

gems.name: Grand Canyon
gems.blurb: A mile-deep gorge of banded rock that humbles everyone who sees it.
gems.tip: Catch sunrise at Mather Point; the quieter North Rim is open in summer only.

gems.name: Yosemite
gems.blurb: Granite giants, giant sequoias and thundering spring waterfalls.
gems.tip: See the falls at peak melt; Glacier Point gives the classic valley view.

gems.name: Yellowstone
gems.blurb: Rainbow hot springs, geysers and roaming bison over a supervolcano.
gems.tip: Grand Prismatic is best from the overlook trail; dawn for wildlife in Lamar.

gems.name: Horseshoe Bend
gems.blurb: The Colorado River carving a perfect emerald horseshoe far below.
gems.tip: Sunset light fills the canyon; it's a short but exposed walk — bring water.

gems.name: Big Sur
gems.blurb: Cliff-hugging Highway 1 where redwoods meet a wild Pacific.
gems.tip: Drive south-to-north for ocean-side views; stop at McWay Falls.

gems.name: New Orleans
gems.blurb: Jazz on every corner and Creole-Cajun cooking that defines a city.
gems.tip: Beignets at dawn, gumbo for lunch, live brass in the Marigny at night.

gems.name: Banff & Lake Louise
gems.blurb: Glacier-fed turquoise lakes beneath the jagged Canadian Rockies.
gems.tip: Sunrise canoe on Lake Louise; nearby Moraine Lake is even bluer.

gems.name: Tulum
gems.blurb: A clifftop Mayan ruin above a turquoise Caribbean beach.
gems.tip: Arrive at opening; cool off in a nearby cenote afterwards.

gems.name: Chichén Itzá
gems.blurb: The colossal Mayan pyramid of Kukulcán on the Yucatán plain.
gems.tip: Be there at opening to beat heat and crowds; pair with cenote Ik Kil.

gems.name: Hierve el Agua
gems.blurb: Petrified mineral waterfalls and infinity spring-pools over a valley.
gems.tip: Go early from Oaxaca; swim in the upper pools before tour groups arrive.

gems.name: Oaxaca
gems.blurb: Mexico's culinary capital — mole, mezcal and vivid markets.
gems.tip: Eat tlayudas at a mercado; visit during Día de Muertos if you can.

gems.name: Lake Atitlán
gems.blurb: A volcano-ringed lake fringed by Maya villages each with its own character.
gems.tip: Hop between San Marcos and San Juan by lancha; sunrise from Indian Nose.

gems.name: Semuc Champey
gems.blurb: Stepped turquoise pools on a natural limestone bridge in the jungle.
gems.tip: Climb to El Mirador for the classic view; the road in is rough.

gems.name: Río Celeste
gems.blurb: A waterfall where two clear rivers meet and turn brilliant sky-blue.
gems.tip: Hike Tenorio early before rain clouds the water; bring waterproof shoes.

gems.name: Monteverde Cloud Forest
gems.blurb: A misty canopy world of hummingbirds, quetzals and hanging bridges.
gems.tip: Dawn walks for wildlife; zip-line above the clouds afterwards.

gems.name: Pyramids of Giza
gems.blurb: The last standing ancient wonder, rising straight from the desert edge of Cairo.
gems.tip: Enter at opening; a camel ride to the panorama point frames all three.

gems.name: Abu Simbel
gems.blurb: Ramses II's colossal rock temples relocated above Lake Nasser.
gems.tip: Fly or convoy from Aswan for the cooler early morning light.

gems.name: Victoria Falls
gems.blurb: The thundering mile-wide curtain the locals call the Smoke That Thunders.
gems.tip: High water for the spectacle, low water to swim Devil's Pool on the edge.

gems.name: Serengeti
gems.blurb: Endless plains hosting the planet's greatest wildlife migration.
gems.tip: Follow the herds seasonally; a balloon safari at dawn is unforgettable.

gems.name: Ngorongoro Crater
gems.blurb: A collapsed volcano cradling one of Africa's densest wildlife arenas.
gems.tip: Descend at sunrise; you can spot the Big Five in a single morning.

gems.name: Mount Kilimanjaro
gems.blurb: Africa's rooftop — a glaciered volcano you can walk to the top of.
gems.tip: Choose a longer route (Lemosho) to acclimatise and boost summit odds.

gems.name: Sossusvlei
gems.blurb: Towering rust-red dunes and the ghostly dead trees of Deadvlei.
gems.tip: Climb Big Daddy at sunrise, then descend into Deadvlei for the photo.

gems.name: Okavango Delta
gems.blurb: A vast inland delta explored by dugout canoe among elephants and hippos.
gems.tip: A mokoro safari at flood peak glides you silently past the wildlife.

gems.name: Lalibela
gems.blurb: Medieval churches hewn downward out of solid rock, still in worship.
gems.tip: Visit at dawn during a service; the cross-shaped Bete Giyorgis is iconic.

gems.name: Danakil Depression
gems.blurb: One of Earth's hottest places — neon sulphur springs and lava lakes.
gems.tip: Only with an organised armed convoy; Erta Ale's lava glows best at night.

gems.name: Zanzibar Stone Town
gems.blurb: A Swahili-Arab maze of carved doors, spice markets and dhow sunsets.
gems.tip: Get lost in the alleys by day; rooftop dinner as the dhows sail out.

gems.name: Avenue of the Baobabs
gems.blurb: Ancient giant baobabs lining a dirt road, glowing at sunset.
gems.tip: Sunset and sunrise both deliver; stay nearby in Morondava.

gems.name: Marrakech
gems.blurb: A sensory blast of souks, riads and the chaos of Jemaa el-Fnaa.
gems.tip: Stay in a riad for calm; rooftop mint tea over the square at dusk.

gems.name: Erg Chebbi Dunes
gems.blurb: Classic Sahara — towering golden dunes you reach by camel from Merzouga.
gems.tip: Overnight in a desert camp; the silence and stars are the point.

gems.name: Petra
gems.blurb: The rose-red city carved into a canyon by the ancient Nabataeans.
gems.tip: Walk the Siq at opening; hike up to the Monastery away from the crowds.

gems.name: Persepolis
gems.blurb: The monumental ceremonial capital of the Persian Empire.
gems.tip: Go late afternoon for warm light on the reliefs; pair with nearby Shiraz.

gems.name: Registan, Samarkand
gems.blurb: Three towering tiled madrasas framing the heart of the Silk Road.
gems.tip: Come back after dark when the mosaics are floodlit.

gems.name: Taj Mahal
gems.blurb: The peerless white-marble mausoleum that glows at first light.
gems.tip: Enter at sunrise; the marble shifts colour through the morning.

gems.name: Hampi
gems.blurb: A surreal landscape of boulders strewn with ruined Vijayanagara temples.
gems.tip: Rent a scooter; sunset from Matanga Hill over the boulder fields.

gems.name: Pangong Lake, Ladakh
gems.blurb: A high-altitude lake that shifts through blues against bare Himalayan peaks.
gems.tip: Acclimatise in Leh first; nights are freezing even in summer.

gems.name: Kerala Backwaters
gems.blurb: A green labyrinth of palm-lined canals cruised by houseboat.
gems.tip: Overnight on a houseboat from Alleppey; smaller boats reach quiet channels.

gems.name: Angkor Wat
gems.blurb: The largest religious monument on Earth, swallowed and freed by jungle.
gems.tip: Sunrise over the towers, then beat crowds to the tree-roots of Ta Prohm.

gems.name: Hạ Long Bay
gems.blurb: Thousands of limestone karsts rising from emerald water.
gems.tip: Overnight cruise into quieter Lan Ha Bay to dodge the crowds.

gems.name: Sapa Rice Terraces
gems.blurb: Staircases of glowing green (or gold) rice terraces in the northern hills.
gems.tip: Trek between hill-tribe villages with a local guide; gold at harvest.

gems.name: Hội An
gems.blurb: A lantern-lit riverside old town and one of Asia's great food stops.
gems.tip: Eat cao lầu and white-rose dumplings; float a lantern on the full moon.

gems.name: Bromo & Ijen
gems.blurb: A smoking caldera at sunrise and a crater that burns electric-blue at night.
gems.tip: Bromo sunrise from King Kong Hill; Ijen's blue flames need a 2am start.

gems.name: Tegallalang, Bali
gems.blurb: Sculpted emerald rice terraces in the cultural heart of Bali.
gems.tip: Arrive at opening; pair with the temples and waterfalls around Ubud.

gems.name: Komodo National Park
gems.blurb: Pink beaches, manta rays and the last wild Komodo dragons.
gems.tip: A liveaboard from Labuan Bajo reaches Padar Island's famous viewpoint.

gems.name: El Nido, Palawan
gems.blurb: Hidden lagoons and limestone cliffs over impossibly clear water.
gems.tip: Island-hop tours A and C; kayak into the Big and Small Lagoons early.

gems.name: Chocolate Hills
gems.blurb: Over a thousand symmetrical hills that turn cocoa-brown in the dry season.
gems.tip: Climb the Carmen viewpoint at sunrise; rent a scooter around Bohol.

gems.name: Sigiriya
gems.blurb: An ancient sky-palace atop a sheer 200m rock, reached by frescoed stairs.
gems.tip: Climb at opening before the heat; the Pidurangala rock opposite frames it.

gems.name: Ella
gems.blurb: Misty tea-country hills, Nine Arch Bridge and easy peak hikes.
gems.tip: Ride the scenic train in; catch a train crossing the bridge.

gems.name: Mount Fuji
gems.blurb: Japan's perfect sacred cone, framed best from the Chureito Pagoda.
gems.tip: Clear winter mornings give the sharpest views; cherry blossoms in spring.

gems.name: Arashiyama Bamboo Grove
gems.blurb: A towering green bamboo corridor on the edge of Kyoto.
gems.tip: Be there by 7am for empty paths; combine with nearby temples.

gems.name: Shirakawa-gō
gems.blurb: Steep-thatched farmhouses in a valley that vanishes under deep snow.
gems.tip: Winter light-up evenings are magical but require advance reservations.

gems.name: Zhangye Danxia
gems.blurb: Rolling hills painted in candy-stripe bands of red, orange and gold.
gems.tip: Late afternoon light deepens the colours; after rain they glow strongest.

gems.name: Jiuzhaigou
gems.blurb: Multicoloured lakes and waterfalls in a forested Tibetan valley.
gems.tip: Autumn foliage doubles the colour; go on a weekday to avoid crowds.

gems.name: Great Wall at Jinshanling
gems.blurb: A wild, crumbling, near-empty stretch of the Great Wall for real hiking.
gems.tip: Day-trip from Beijing; far quieter than Badaling, with sunrise mist.

gems.name: Pokhara & Annapurna
gems.blurb: Lakeside launchpad to Himalayan treks under Machapuchare's fishtail peak.
gems.tip: Sunrise from Sarangkot; the Poon Hill trek is a doable taster.

gems.name: Khongoryn Els
gems.blurb: The singing sand dunes of the Gobi, climbed for a vast desert sunset.
gems.tip: Sleep in a ger camp; ride a Bactrian camel to the dune base.

gems.name: Tbilisi
gems.blurb: Sulphur baths, cliff-top churches and the feast of Georgian wine country.
gems.tip: Eat khinkali and khachapuri; day-trip to the Kazbegi mountains.

gems.name: Cinque Terre
gems.blurb: Five candy-coloured fishing villages clinging to the Ligurian cliffs.
gems.tip: Hike the coastal trail between villages; trains link them if it's closed.

gems.name: Dolomites — Tre Cime
gems.blurb: Three monolithic peaks circled by one of the Alps' great day hikes.
gems.tip: The loop is easy and stunning; start early for parking and light.

gems.name: Lake Como
gems.blurb: An elegant alpine lake of villa gardens and lakeside villages.
gems.tip: Ferry-hop between Varenna, Bellagio and Menaggio; skip the car.

gems.name: Santorini
gems.blurb: Whitewashed cliff towns above a flooded volcanic caldera.
gems.tip: Watch sunset from Oia early; explore quieter Pyrgos and the east coast.

gems.name: Sintra
gems.blurb: Fairytale palaces and mossy gardens in the hills above Lisbon.
gems.tip: Book Pena Palace first slot; explore the romantic Quinta da Regaleira well.

gems.name: Sete Cidades, Azores
gems.blurb: Twin crater lakes — one blue, one green — in a volcanic caldera.
gems.tip: Hike or bike the rim; weather turns fast, so go on a clear morning.

gems.name: Mont Saint-Michel
gems.blurb: A medieval abbey-island that the tide cuts off from the mainland.
gems.tip: Stay overnight to see it floodlit and empty; watch the fast tide come in.

gems.name: Verdon Gorge
gems.blurb: Europe's grand canyon — turquoise river beneath towering limestone walls.
gems.tip: Kayak the gorge from Lac de Sainte-Croix; drive the cliff-rim route.

gems.name: Lauterbrunnen
gems.blurb: A glacial valley with 72 waterfalls plunging from sheer walls.
gems.tip: Base here for the Jungfrau region; cable-car up to car-free Mürren.

gems.name: Zermatt & Matterhorn
gems.blurb: The iconic pyramid peak above a car-free alpine village.
gems.tip: Sunrise reflection of the Matterhorn in Riffelsee; hike the Gornergrat.

gems.name: Jökulsárlón
gems.blurb: A glacier lagoon of drifting icebergs beside a black Diamond Beach.
gems.tip: Combine with the South Coast waterfalls; watch for seals among the ice.

gems.name: Reynisfjara
gems.blurb: A dramatic black-sand beach of basalt columns and sea stacks.
gems.tip: Respect the sneaker waves — never turn your back on the surf.

gems.name: Giant's Causeway
gems.blurb: 40,000 interlocking basalt columns stepping into the North Atlantic.
gems.tip: Walk the cliff path for the wide view; early or late dodges the buses.

gems.name: Isle of Skye
gems.blurb: Jagged ridges, fairy pools and the otherworldly Quiraing landslip.
gems.tip: Hike the Quiraing loop; weather changes hourly, so go flexible.

gems.name: Cliffs of Moher
gems.blurb: 214m Atlantic cliffs alive with seabirds on Ireland's wild west.
gems.tip: Walk the coastal trail beyond the visitor centre for solitude.

gems.name: Český Krumlov
gems.blurb: A storybook medieval town wrapped in a bend of the Vltava river.
gems.tip: Climb the castle tower at golden hour; raft the river loop in summer.

gems.name: Rila Monastery
gems.blurb: A vividly frescoed Orthodox monastery cradled in forested mountains.
gems.tip: Day-trip from Sofia; arrive early for quiet courtyards and frescoes.

gems.name: Bran & Transylvania
gems.blurb: Castle-topped hills and Saxon villages in the Carpathian heartland.
gems.tip: Drive the Transfăgărășan road in summer; base in Brașov.

gems.name: Geirangerfjord
gems.blurb: A deep blue fjord walled by waterfalls and abandoned mountain farms.
gems.tip: Kayak the fjord or drive the Eagle Road for the classic overlook.

gems.name: Trolltunga
gems.blurb: A thin rock tongue jutting over a fjord 700m below.
gems.tip: A long 10-12hr return hike — start early and check trail status.

gems.name: Uluru
gems.blurb: A sacred monolith glowing red at the heart of the outback.
gems.tip: Watch the colour change at sunset; walk the base loop with a guide.

gems.name: Great Barrier Reef
gems.blurb: The largest living structure on Earth, teeming with coral and turtles.
gems.tip: Snorkel the outer reef from Port Douglas; choose eco-certified operators.

gems.name: Twelve Apostles
gems.blurb: Limestone sea stacks marching off the wild Great Ocean Road.
gems.tip: Sunset from Gibson Steps; drive the whole road over two days.

gems.name: Whitehaven Beach
gems.blurb: Blindingly white silica sand swirling into Whitsunday blues.
gems.tip: Hill Inlet lookout for the swirl; sail in from Airlie Beach.

gems.name: Milford Sound
gems.blurb: A sheer-walled fjord of waterfalls and seals in Fiordland.
gems.tip: It rains often — and that's when the waterfalls are best; early cruise.

gems.name: Roys Peak, Wānaka
gems.blurb: A switchback climb to a ridgeline view over Lake Wānaka's islands.
gems.tip: Pre-dawn start to catch sunrise and beat the queue at the viewpoint.

gems.name: Tongariro Crossing
gems.blurb: A volcanic day-hike past steaming craters and Emerald Lakes.
gems.tip: Check the weather religiously; arrange the shuttle in advance.

gems.name: Bora Bora
gems.blurb: An emerald peak ringed by a turquoise lagoon and overwater bungalows.
gems.tip: Snorkel the coral gardens; circle the motu by boat at sunset.

gems.name: Aitutaki
gems.blurb: One of the South Pacific's most perfect lagoons, barely touched.
gems.tip: Take a lagoon cruise to One Foot Island's sandbar.

gems.name: Penang — George Town
gems.blurb: A heritage port city famous for street art and legendary hawker food.
gems.tip: Eat char kway teow and laksa at the stalls; explore the clan jetties.

gems.name: Bangkok — Chinatown
gems.blurb: Yaowarat's neon night-market of woks, noodles and durian.
gems.tip: Go hungry after dark; graze stall to stall down the side sois.

gems.name: Istanbul
gems.blurb: Where continents meet — domes, bazaars and Bosphorus ferries.
gems.tip: Dawn at the Blue Mosque, sunset ferry to the Asian side for dinner.

gems.name: San Sebastián
gems.blurb: A Basque beach city with the world's densest run of pintxos bars.
gems.tip: Bar-hop the old town for pintxos; one or two bites per bar.

gems.name: Lima — Barranco
gems.blurb: A bohemian clifftop district anchoring one of the world's great food scenes.
gems.tip: Ceviche at lunch, world-ranked tasting menus by night.
